channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Wanted BIFF faction leader killed in police-military operation in Maguindanao del Norte
item.description: Esmael Abubakar, also known as Kumander Bungos and identified as a leader of a faction of the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters (BIFF), was killed in a joint police and military operation in Sultan ...

item.title: JM de Guzman Discusses Portraying Victor Zafra in 'Coco Martin’s Sigabo'
item.description: The article explores JM de Guzman's experience in portraying Victor Zafra, the newest antagonist in 'Coco Martin’s Sigabo.' As a seasoned actor, de Guzman shares insights into the challenges of bringi...

item.title: Padel's Rapid Growth and Its Impact on Recreational Lawn Tennis
item.description: Padel, a sport that combines elements of tennis and squash, is played on a smaller walled court with a net. Invented in Mexico in 1969, it has gained massive popularity especially in Spain and Latin A...

item.title: Jason Canela's Career Highlights Ahead of His Starring Role in the Musical 'On Your Feet'
item.description: The article introduces Cuban-American actor Jason Canela, who stars in the musical production 'On Your Feet', and gives readers a brief look at his background and career. While promoting his participa...

item.title: Philippine Tennis Star Eala Defeats Wimbledon Champion Swiatek, Rybakina Also Eliminated in Wimbledon Third Round
item.description: Philippine tennis player Alexandra Eala made history by defeating Wimbledon champion Iga Swiatek 7-6 (9), 6-2 in the third round of the 2026 Wimbledon tournament, becoming the first Filipino to reach ...

item.title: Ebonyi State Launches Panel to Enforce Ban on Unauthorized School Ceremonies
item.description: The Ebonyi State Government has established an investigative panel to address schools violating the ban on hosting graduation and valedictory ceremonies without official approval. This follows reports...

item.title: Real Madrid signs Denzel Dumfries on four-year deal
item.description: Real Madrid have finalized the transfer of Netherlands defender Denzel Dumfries from Inter Milan for a reported €20 million fee. The 30-year-old right-back, who played for the Netherlands in the 2026 ...

item.title: U.S. official defends free speech amid white supremacist protest in Washington
item.description: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum downplayed concerns about a July Fourth protest by white supremacists in Washington, calling their actions a 'messy' aspect of democracy protected by free speech. The ev...

item.title: Naomi Osaka reaches first Wimbledon quarter-final as Aryna Sabalenka exits after straight-sets defeat
item.description: Naomi Osaka produced one of the standout performances of the Wimbledon championships by defeating world number one Aryna Sabalenka 6-2, 7-6 to reach the quarter-finals of the tournament for the first ...

item.title: Djokovic advances to Wimbledon quarterfinals after historic win, Osaka defeats top seed Sabalenka
item.description: Novak Djokovic extended his Wimbledon legacy by defeating Russian qualifier Roman Safiullin 7-6(6) 6-3 3-6 6-3, securing his 106th match victory at the All England Club and surpassing Roger Federer's ...

item.title: Explaining India's E20 petrol rollout: Mileage impact, vehicle compatibility and the road ahead
item.description: India's nationwide rollout of E20 petrol, which contains 20% ethanol blended with petrol, has sparked renewed debate among consumers, automobile manufacturers and policymakers. Although the government...

item.title: Police officer surrenders after being linked to Zamboanga del Sur policeman’s killing; former cop also gives himself up in separate murder case
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) announced that significant progress has been made in the investigation into the fatal shooting of Police Corporal Armejo Jornadal in Dumalinao, Zamboanga del Sur o...

item.title: LAPD Officer Shoots Dog Wearing Knicks T-Shirt, Family Sues City
item.description: A 2-year-old golden Saint Berdoodle named Jameson was fatally shot by an LAPD officer on June 13 while wearing a New York Knicks T-shirt. Marie Marseille, the dog’s owner, filed a civil rights lawsuit...

item.title: Thousands gather in Saskatoon to watch Canada’s historic World Cup round of 16 match against Morocco
item.description: A large public gathering in Saskatoon brought together thousands of soccer fans to watch Canada’s Round of 16 World Cup match against Morocco, marking a historic moment for the national team. The even...

item.title: Police say security remains stable as investigations continue into alleged plans for unlawful demonstrations and violence
item.description: The Tanzania Police Force has assured wananchi that the country's security situation remains calm and that adequate measures have been put in place to protect lives and property. Speaking to journalis...

item.title: Malatsi details two approaches by Resolve Communications and denies firm arranged Starlink meeting
item.description: Communications Minister Solly Malatsi has informed Parliament that Resolve Communications, a public relations firm chaired by former DA leader Tony Leon, contacted his office on two separate occasions...

item.title: Andi Abaya and LA Santos Compete in Word Hunt Challenge on 'Miss Behave'
item.description: The article discusses the entertaining rivalry between actors Andi Abaya and LA Santos as they participate in a word hunt challenge on the TV show 'Miss Behave.' The competition, described as hilariou...

item.title: Tim Ream says the U.S. World Cup squad reflects America's diversity ahead of Belgium quarterfinal
item.description: U.S. men's national soccer team defender Tim Ream said the team's diverse makeup reflects the identity of the United States as it prepares for a crucial World Cup match against Belgium. Speaking on th...

item.title: Socceroos' World Cup Journey: Heartbreak and Hope for Future Success
item.description: The Socceroos' World Cup campaign ended in heartbreak after a penalty shootout loss to Egypt, but their young squad and diverse team composition signal promising future prospects. Despite falling shor...

item.title: Tanzania's Dumisha Amani Project Empowers Youth Through Vocational Training and Entrepreneurship
item.description: The Dumisha Amani Project, funded by the United Nations Peace and Development Fund (UNPDF), United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Tanzania, and the Centre for Peace and Prosperity (CPP), is prov...
